mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 220) triggers central bank tightening, increasing real interest rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, or inflation-adjusted earnings growth outperforms rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO > 110) and GDP (GDPC1 > 11700) signals robust supply-side expansion, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined effect of INDPRO and GDPC1 fails to produce positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening (UNRATE < 5%) increases wage pressures and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE is below 5%, or wage growth fails to outpace inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 1000) indicate elevated demand for residential construction, stimulating related sectors and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 1000, or housing data fails to correlate with sector-specific earnings.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 130k) and rising unemployment (UNRATE > 5.5%) signals labor market weakness, reducing consumer confidence and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE interaction fails to predict negative returns, or the combined effect's p-value exceeds 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 380k) reflects strong demand for goods and services, increasing corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS exceeds 380k, or retail sales data fails to correlate with equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high Fed funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 3%) and low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2%) indicates a tightening cycle, increasing borrowing costs and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction fails to predict negative returns, or the combined effect's p-value exceeds 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.
